title: TIME: Veteran ICE Agents Say They Are 'Embarrassed' by Minneapolis Tactics
excerpt: Current and former ICE agents with decades of experience told TIME magazine they were troubled by the fatal shooting of Renee Good and broader agency conduct in Minneapolis. One 25-year veteran said: 'The majority of my colleagues feel the same way. It's an insult to us.'
content:
In the week following the fatal shooting of Renee Good, TIME magazine spoke with current and former ICE agents who expressed serious reservations about the agency's conduct in Minneapolis and the broader use-of-force trajectory under the Trump administration.

  • A former agent with more than 25 years of experience said colleagues found the shooting of Good an 'insult' to those who had 'done it the right way.'
  • Both agents interviewed questioned why Agent Jonathan Ross was assigned to the Minneapolis surge given his documented history of a traumatic dragging incident months prior involving a vehicle — raising fitness-for-duty concerns.
  • A 20-year active ICE veteran acknowledged the shooting was 'very problematic,' saying that while policy technically allows fire at a vehicle if an officer fears for their life, the video evidence made Ross's justification difficult to sustain.

The TIME report added to a growing body of internal dissent about the Trump administration's aggressive push to expand the agent workforce and the operational culture it was fostering.
